title: Aptitude: Trigonometry & Height and Distances - Q7

content.problemStatement:
### **Aptitude Practice Question**

The shadow of a tree decreases by 15m when the sun’s altitude changes from 45° to 60°. Find the length of the tree.Let AB be the length of the tree and the length of shadow be x when the angle of elevation is 45°.<figca

---

### **Answer & Explanation**

ption> Then, the length of shadow when the angle of elevation is 60° = x – 15m

For angle 45° in triangle ABD, we have
AB/BD = 1/1 = AB/x
So, AB = x

Now, for angle 60° in triangle ABC, we have

AB/BC = √3/1 = x/(x – 15)
⇒ x = √3x – 15√3
⇒ (√3 – 1)x = 15√3
⇒ x = 15√3/(√3 – 1)m

The length of the tree is 15√3/(√3 – 1)m.
